top_blockers: No formal Privacy Policy, Terms of Use, or Cookie Notice surfaced in product
Cookie/storage consent posture is incomplete for a UK/EU-facing launch
AWS backup, restore, alerting, and secret rotation readiness are documented but not evidenced from repo alone
Verify-customer bearer compatibility and auth cutover are not fully enforced
Connector signing and notarization status must be confirmed for distributed installer flows
top_post_launch_risks: Failed clean recovery or greenfield environment provisioning
Privacy complaints or sales friction due to missing disclosures
Manufacturer distrust of unsigned or weakly trusted connector packages
Operational weakness if backup or restore evidence is not current
Support burden caused by mixed legacy and new auth behavior
must_fix_before_launch: Repair and validate Prisma migration chain in a clean database
Publish and surface privacy, terms, and cookie documents
Finalize cookie/storage consent approach and implement needed UI
Collect AWS evidence for backups, restore drills, SSL, alerts, and storage controls
Run role-based UAT and onboarding signoff
can_wait_until_post_launch: Additional polish of low-risk empty and error states
Advanced enterprise controls such as SSO/SCIM and customer-facing audit exports

package_signing_decision.distributed_connector: Required or strongly recommended for Windows signing and macOS signing/notarization
